Feature: Custom Geometry GPU pipeline contact reports
Description of Limitation: Custom geometry contact data is not made available via the tensor API GPU contact information getters.
Recommended Workaround: Use GPU native collision approximation like convex hull, SDF tri-mesh, base geoms like sphere/box/capsule. Disable custom geometry cylinders and cones in physics stage settings.
Perf Impact Reason: Contacts are generated by CPU custom geometry callbacks

Feature: Custom Geometry collision against GPU Features
Description of Limitation: Custom geometry does not collide with GPU features (particles and deformable bodies), and may have poor collision quality against SDF tri-mesh colliders.
Recommended Workaround: Use GPU native collision approximation like convex hull, SDF tri-mesh, base geoms like sphere/box/capsule. Disable custom geometry cylinders and cones in physics stage settings.
Perf Impact Reason: Contacts are generated by CPU custom geometry callbacks

Feature: Particles and deformable body contact reports
Description of Limitation: Particles and deformable body do not support contact reports
Recommended Workaround: NA

Description of Limitation: Replaying a simulation from an in-contact simulation state saved out in the middle of a simulation run can be nondeterministic. The PhysX SDK is using internal contact state that can persist over multiple simulation steps and that cannot currently be serialized and recovered from USD data.
Recommended Workaround: Restart simulations from the beginning to achieve determinism.

Feature: Conveyor Belts / Kinematics with nonzero velocity
Description of Limitation:
- Deformable bodies and particles do not support conveyor belts and will not contact/fall through. 
- Collision behavior between conveyor belts and SDF tri-mesh dynamics is inadequate
Recommended Workaround: Do not use the conveyor belt feature with particles, deformable bodies, or SDF tri-mesh collision geometry. Use rigid bodies with non-SDF collision geometry instead.
Perf Impact Reason: The conveyor belt feature will trigger a CPU code path, so it is best to avoid for maximum GPU pipeline performance.

Description of Limitation: Isosurface may be leaking memory
Recommended Workaround: Do not use isosurface feature if memory leaks are an issue. It is a render-only feature and does not affect underlying fluid simulation.
Perf Impact Reason: Memory leak will lead to out of memory (OOM)

Feature: Deformable Bodies and Particles static friction
Description of Limitation: Static friction is not supported.
Recommended Workaround: NA

Feature: Deformable Bodies and Particle Friction Combine Mode
Description of Limitation: Friction combine mode is not supported. Any interaction is using the dynamic friction set on the particle/deformable actor.
Recommended Workaround: NA

Description of Limitation: Particles can roll off flat collision surfaces that are perpendicular to gravity due to solver ghost forces.
Recommended Workaround: NA

Description of Limitation: The simulation fidelity and behavior can be inadequate. Articulation joint incoming force reported will be excessively high when using nonzero TGS velocity iterations.
Recommended Workaround:
Fixed tendons: Use the `Mimic Joint feature <https://docs.omniverse.nvidia.com/kit/docs/omni_usd_schema_physics/latest/class_physx_schema_physx_mimic_joint_a_p_i.html>`_.
Spatial tendons: Apply external forces to the links to mimic the spatial tendons.
Force sensing: Use TGS with zero velocity iterations.

Description of Limitation: For performance/memory footprint, the SDK limits GPU-compatible convex hulls to 64 vertices and faces. This can lead to poor approximation quality to the asset collider tri-mesh.
Recommended Workaround: Use convex decomposition or SDF tri-meshes to capture details better. Be aware that a convex decomposition with the same set of vertices as a single convex hull may not produce the exact same behavior because contact detection runs on each convex independently.
Perf Impact Reason: Switching to convex decomposition or SDF will have a simulation perf impact due to higher computational cost.

Feature: Spherical Articulation Joints on links with nonidentity center-of-mass transform (cmasslocalpose)
Description of Limitation: Joint limits and drives for spherical articulation joints may not respond correctly in certain joint state ranges if the articulation joint is setup on a link with nonidentity mass frame setup (cmasslocalpose)
Recommended Workaround: If possible, transform asset such that prim and mass frame coincide such that an identity transform can be used for the mass frame.

Description of Limitation: - With the current release (2023.1), the PhysX SDK no longer silently converts velocity iterations exceeding four to position iterations. Omniverse Physics will issue a corresponding warning to the log. This changing of iteration counts can affect physics behavior.
Recommended Workaround:
- manually converting the velocity iterations >4 to position iterations in assets to recover the behavior before the fix
- try setting zero or very few velocity iterations
- try the PGS solver

Description of Limitation: D6 Joint Drive does not behave exactly as expected when the TGS solver is employed.
Recommended Workaround: If there are drive behavior issues then use PGS instead.

Description of Limitation: D6 Joint Drive does not work well with a combination of TGS and velocity iterations.
Recommended Workaround: When TGS is employed it is recommended to focus computational effort on position iterations and to have zero velocity iterations.

Description of Limitation: The force sensors are deprecated and will be removed in a future version. They reported incorrect/implausible values for many use cases.
Recommended Workaround: Use contact force reports or link incoming joint force instead.

Description of Limitation: The joint solver force reporting is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. The reported forces were incorrect/implausible.
Recommended Workaround: Use link incoming joint force reporting instead.

Description of Limitation:
Particle cloth will be deprecated when replaced with surface deformable bodies when they are ready.
We don't have an ETA for this yet.
Recommended Workaround: Do not use particle cloth.

Description of Limitation: We have seen issues with loop-closures using D6 Joints where the simulation goes unstable, mainly using GPU simulation.
Recommended Workaround: Try increasing simulation time steps per second on the scene (i.e. decrease the simulation time step), try increasing articulation solver iterations, and try PGS and TGS solvers.

Description of Limitation:
There are reports of differing effective joint friction between PGS and TGS solver.

The friction model may not be suitable for all applications,see details in the `API documentation <https://nvidia-omniverse.github.io/PhysX/physx/5.3.0/_api_build/class_px_articulation_joint_reduced_coordinate.html#_CPPv4N36PxArticulationJointReducedCoordinate22setFrictionCoefficientEK6PxReal>`_.
Recommended Workaround: A more common velocity-proportional dynamic friction model can be implemented using a joint drive with zero target velocity and a suitable damping parameter.
